items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager` **Service directory:** `sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/` **Triggered by:** PR #58123 by `@m-nash` ## What Changed PR #58123 added four new public extension methods on `IHostApplicationBuilder` in a new file `ArmClientHostExtensions.cs`: - `AddArmClient(string sectionName)` — registers a singleton `ArmClient` from configuration - `AddArmClient(string sectionName, Action configureSettings)` — same with settings callback - `AddKeyedArmClient(string key, string sectionName)` — registers a keyed singleton `ArmClient` - `AddKeyedArmClient(string key, string sectionName, Action configureSettings)` — same with settings callback These are marked `[Experimental("SCME0002")]` and delegate to the generic `AddAzureClient` extensions from `System.ClientModel`. ## Gaps Found - **CHANGELOG** — `1.15.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` has an empty `### Features Added` section; the new `AddArmClient`/`AddKeyedArmClient` APIs are not listed - **README** — No section or example shows how to use `AddArmClient`/`AddKeyedArmClient` to register `ArmClient` via dependency injection in a .NET host application - **No sample file** — There is no `tests/Samples/` file covering the `IHostApplicationBuilder` registration pattern, so there is nothing for the snippet tool to extract into the README
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File 1: `sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/CHANGELOG.md`** — Edit Add under `## 1.15.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` → `### Features Added`: ```markdown - Added `AddArmClient` and `AddKeyedArmClient` extension methods on `IHostApplicationBuilder` to register `ArmClient` as a singleton via dependency injection. These methods are experimental (`SCME0002`) and delegate to `AddAzureClient` / `AddKeyedAzureClient`. ``` **File 2: `sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/README.md`** — Edit Add a new subsection **"Register with dependency injection"** under the existing `## Getting started` → `### Authenticate the Client` section (or as a new top-level `## Dependency Injection` section near the Examples). Use a `C# Snippet:ArmClientHostExtensions_AddArmClient` placeholder that the snippet tool will populate: ````markdown ## Dependency Injection `Azure.ResourceManager` supports registering `ArmClient` in a .NET host application via `IHostApplicationBuilder`. This feature is experimental. ### Register ArmClient ```C# Snippet:ArmClientHostExtensions_AddArmClient ``` ### Register a Keyed ArmClient ```C# Snippet:ArmClientHostExtensions_AddKeyedArmClient ``` ```` **File 3: `sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/tests/Samples/Sample_ArmClientHostExtensions.cs`** — Create ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis; using Azure.ResourceManager; using Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.ResourceManager.Tests.Samples { public class Sample_ArmClientHostExtensions { [Test] [Experimental("SCME0002")] public void AddArmClientExample() { #region Snippet:ArmClientHostExtensions_AddArmClient // Call AddArmClient on an IHostApplicationBuilder to register ArmClient // as a singleton from the "ArmClient" configuration section. IHostApplicationBuilder builder = Host.CreateApplicationBuilder(); builder.AddArmClient("ArmClient"); IHost host = builder.Build(); // Resolve ArmClient from DI ArmClient client = host.Services.GetRequiredService(); #endregion #if !SNIPPET Assert.IsNotNull(client); host.Dispose(); #endif } [Test] [Experimental("SCME0002")] public void AddKeyedArmClientExample() { #region Snippet:ArmClientHostExtensions_AddKeyedArmClient // Call AddKeyedArmClient to register a keyed ArmClient singleton. IHostApplicationBuilder builder = Host.CreateApplicationBuilder(); builder.AddKeyedArmClient("myKey", "ArmClient"); IHost host = builder.Build(); // Resolve the keyed ArmClient from DI ArmClient client = host.Services.GetRequiredKeyedService("myKey"); #endregion #if !SNIPPET Assert.IsNotNull(client); host.Dispose(); #endif } } } ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets The snippet names are: | Snippet Name | Region in test file | README placeholder location | |---|---|---| | `ArmClientHostExtensions_AddArmClient` | `Sample_ArmClientHostExtensions.AddArmClientExample` | Under new "Register ArmClient" subsection | | `ArmClientHostExtensions_AddKeyedArmClient` | `Sample_ArmClientHostExtensions.AddKeyedArmClientExample` | Under new "Register a Keyed ArmClient" subsection | ### Step 3: Verify README structure Current README sections in order: 1. ✅ Getting started (Install, Prerequisites, Authenticate the Client) 2. ✅ Key concepts 3. ✅ Examples 4. ❌ **Missing:** Dependency Injection / `AddArmClient` registration example 5. ✅ (Troubleshooting / Next steps / Contributing — verify these exist and are complete) Add the new "Dependency Injection" section (with snippet placeholders) as described in Step 1. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next. 1. `dotnet build sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 resourcemanager` — confirms README placeholders are populated 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 resourcemanager` — no public API changes expected (API was already exported in PR #58123), but run to confirm 5. `dotnet format sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
items.title: [ResourceManager] Docs: Document AddArmClient IHostApplicationBuilder extensions
